seoTitle: How to Personalize Emails in Brevo
metaDescription: Add dynamic name tags and merge fields to Brevo campaigns using contact attributes, fallback text, and conditional content blocks for cleaner sends.
intro: Sending cold, generic broadcast emails to your entire audience misses an easy opportunity to boost engagement. Brevo's native template language lets you insert a recipient's first name, location, or other custom details automatically, without manually typing out separate drafts for each subscriber.

steps.heading: Add name tags to subject lines
steps.body: Insert data placeholders into your subject line and preview text to boost early engagement and maximize open rates.

steps.heading: Inject placeholders into the email body
steps.body: Place responsive personalization tags directly into your visual content blocks so the recipient's details display automatically.

steps.heading: Set fallback text for missing data
steps.body: Configure default fallback values for contacts missing a specific field, which prevents broken empty brackets from showing up in the sent email.

steps.heading: Use conditional content blocks
steps.body: Set up rules so different content displays depending on a recipient's attributes, tailoring the message further.

steps.heading: Validate rendering before sending
steps.body: Preview or test-send the campaign to confirm personalization tags display correctly rather than showing raw placeholder syntax.

faq.question: What happens if a contact is missing the data for a merge tag?
faq.answer: If you've set a fallback default text, that displays instead of a broken or empty placeholder.

faq.question: Can I personalize the subject line, not just the body?
faq.answer: Yes, merge tags can be placed directly into subject headers and preview text as well as the email body.

faq.question: What syntax does Brevo use for personalization?
faq.answer: Brevo relies on its native Template Language syntax to swap placeholder variables with live subscriber attributes.

faq.question: Can I show different content to different segments in one email?
faq.answer: Yes, conditional content block rules let you tailor sections of the email based on recipient attributes.
